description: Fork-only expected-failure baseline for the unit suite. Consumed by scripts/test-gate.mjs, which runs each test file in its OWN `bun test` process (isolation). Entries are keyed by { file, exact bun testcase name } as reported by that per-file run. Restores auto-gating despite pre-existing, environment-specific failures inherited from upstream v13.11.0 (ADR 0002 §9, Backlog #6/#7). Retire an entry ONLY when the gate reports it as an 'unexpected pass' (the test now passes). Re-measured 2026-07-17 on Windows 11 / Bun 1.3.5.
notes: EXECUTION MODEL: the gate runs each file in its own `bun test <file>` process, not one combined `bun test <all>` process. Two measured facts forced this (see PR body): (1) a combined run intermittently panics ('We should be either stdout or stderr') when a test spawns a child process under --reporter=junit — a ~1/3 flaky crash rate that would make the gate falsely report 'suite did not complete'; per-file isolation + retry contains it. (2) The combined run's failing set is TOPOLOGY-DEPENDENT: on Windows, an earlier test's `process.env = {...}` reassignment replaces the case-insensitive env proxy with a plain object, so a later `process.env.ComSpec` read yields undefined and the #6 spawn-contract assertions flip from fail to pass depending on run order. Per-file isolation is deterministic and topology-independent, and reproduces the plan's per-file baseline exactly.

#6 (spawn-contract-windows, 3 tests): baselined as win32 expected-failures per the coordinator directive (Mark decision (d): encode now, upstream later). Root cause: src/shared/spawn.ts returns `process.env.ComSpec ?? 'cmd.exe'` and on Windows ComSpec is the absolute cmd.exe path, so `toBe('cmd.exe')` fails. Cheaply fixable in principle (`toBe(process.env.ComSpec ?? 'cmd.exe')`) but the file is upstream-identical, so an in-fork edit manufactures per-sync divergence (ADR 0002 §9) — deferred to option (b): upstream the assertion fix. When that lands, the gate's unexpected-pass ratchet flags these 3 entries for removal.

nonRunnable.reason: Hangs during bun initialization (banner only, no file header); pure file, reproduces in isolation. Excluded so the suite completes. Root-cause investigation deferred (see Queue follow-up).
Hangs (pass=0/fail=0, 75s), reproduces in isolation. Excluded so the suite completes.

bun exits code 3 and the JUnit reporter never writes its XML, breaking the gate for the whole run. Underlying failures are Windows-path artifacts in the test's own expected-value logic (home.split('/') on C:\Users\...). Excluded so the reporter emits output.

expectedFailures.name: shared spawn wrapper wraps .cmd shims with cmd.exe and windowsHide
expectedFailures.reason: #6 upstream-owned: toBe('cmd.exe') vs spawn.ts:74 `process.env.ComSpec ?? 'cmd.exe'` (ComSpec = absolute cmd.exe path on Windows). Mark decision (spec B): default (d) baseline; consider (b) upstream the fix. See notes[1].

expectedFailures.name: wraps .cmd shims with cmd.exe /d /s /c and one quoted command string without shell:true
wraps the codex.cmd fallback with cmd.exe /d /s /c without shell:true
purges observer-sessions and stuck pending_messages, writes marker, wipes chroma
expectedFailures.reason: afterEach rmSync EBUSY — open SQLite handle not released before delete (Windows lock). Upstream-owned.

expectedFailures.name: preserves pending_messages when stuck count is below the threshold of 10
is idempotent: a second invocation does no work and does not create a second backup
proceeds with cleanup when statfsSync returns non-credible values (Bun darwin-x64 #31133)
honors CLAUDE_MEM_SKIP_CLEANUP_V12_4_3=1 by exiting without writing the marker
should resolve relative paths using projectRoot
expectedFailures.reason: relative-path resolution via OS separator. Upstream-owned.

expectedFailures.name: should handle projectRoot with trailing slash correctly
should deduplicate relative paths from same folder with projectRoot
should handle empty string paths gracefully with projectRoot
should process other folders even when one has active CLAUDE.md
expectedFailures.reason: issue #859 folder handling, Windows paths. Upstream-owned.

expectedFailures.name: should skip only the specific folder containing active CLAUDE.md
expectedFailures.reason: issue #859 folder handling, Windows paths. Upstream-owned.

expectedFailures.name: should skip folder when either CLAUDE.md or CLAUDE.local.md was read
expectedFailures.reason: CLAUDE.local.md folder handling, Windows paths. Upstream-owned.

expectedFailures.name: derives transcriptPath from cwd + conversation_id when the file exists (Bug A regression)
expectedFailures.reason: deriveCursorTranscriptPath path derivation uses \ on Windows. Upstream-owned.

expectedFailures.name: returns transcriptPath: undefined when the file does not exist
returns undefined when sessionId is missing (deriveCursorTranscriptPath direct call)
returns undefined when cwd is missing (deriveCursorTranscriptPath direct call)
should return first modified file as relative path
should return relative path (not absolute) for files inside cwd
falls back to known install locations when PATH has no claude
expectedFailures.reason: Windows install-location fallback differs from fixture. Upstream-owned.

expectedFailures.name: should look up Bun on non-Windows when caller is Node (e.g. MCP server)
expectedFailures.reason: resolveWorkerRuntimePath path.join yields \ on Windows; injected pathExists matches only the / string. Upstream-owned.

expectedFailures.name: preserves the destination file mode when the file already exists
expectedFailures.reason: expect(mode).toBe(0o600) — Windows does not represent POSIX mode. Upstream-owned.

expectedFailures.name: kills the prior subprocess tree before a reconnect spawn
expectedFailures.reason: killProcessTree spy does not intercept (killTreeCalls stays []); same mock-does-not-intercept class as PR #21's net.createServer. Also slow. Upstream-owned.

expectedFailures.name: stop() disposes state including any pending connecting promise
stop() during a hanging prewarm does not record uvx unavailable or apply reconnect backoff
uses the configured prewarm timeout before constructing transport and kills the prewarm tree
should NOT use console.log/console.error (these logs are invisible in background services)
expectedFailures.reason: PLATFORM-INDEPENDENT source-standard assertion: 26 upstream-owned src files use console.log outside src/hooks/. Fixing = editing 26 upstream files; out of scope. Red on Linux too.

expectedFailures.name: routes all IO through hook-io.ts and no longer blanket-swallows stderr
expectedFailures.reason: PLATFORM-INDEPENDENT source-standard assertion (hookCommand stderr discipline). Upstream source. Red on Linux too.

expectedFailures.name: resolves _P from the cache directory when CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT is unset
expectedFailures.reason: FORK-OWNED (Rule-A shell resolution). Task 5 decision = KEEP (not fix-at-source): the bash hook resolves the correct cache dir but returns a POSIX path (RESOLVED=/tmp/.../cache/thedotmack/claude-mem/99.0.0) while the test builds a Windows-backslash expectation via Node path.join under MSYS — a test-side separator artifact, not a runtime bug (hooks run under bash, so POSIX paths are correct at runtime). Editing this security-sensitive exact-path assertion to normalise separators risks weakening it; deferred as an optional fork-owned follow-up. Fork-owned ⇒ this entry carries zero divergence cost.

conditionalFailures.name: the R2 summary-lane privacy net (markPrivateSession) actually executed this run
conditionalFailures.reason: #35 privacy sentinel (PR #37): intentional red when the URL is unset (7 Postgres integration tests skip); green when set (they run). Env-conditional by design — must NOT be hard-failed.
